input.background: In the worked example of regression analysis (section 11.10.1), the $W^{\prime}$ test for non-Normality of the residuals gave $\mathrm{P}=0.03$. Using the data in Table 11.6, analyze the relationship between $\log _{\mathrm{e}} \mathrm{Vcf}$ and blood glucose, assess the Normality of residuals, and compare prediction intervals for a diabetic patient with a fasting blood glucose of $16 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{l}$.
input.question: Are the residuals from this analysis more nearly Normal?

output.answer: The test of Normality of the residuals from this regression line gives $W^{\prime}=0.936, \mathrm{P}>0.1$. The residuals after log transformation are thus more nearly Normal than those from the analysis of the raw data.
